changed_functions.name: SymCryptEckeySetValue (sub_180041b98) / SymCryptEckeySetValue (sub_180041b50)
key_changes: Replaced a hardcoded public-key element-count doubling ('mov eax,[rsi+14h]; add eax,eax' at 0x180041c6d-0x180041c70) with a lookup into the pre-existing SymCryptEcpointFormatNumberofElements table ('movsxd rax,[rsp+arg]; mov eax, ds:rva SymCryptEcpointFormatNumberofElements[rcx+rax*4]; imul eax,[rsi+14h]' at 0x180041c29-0x180041c38, with rcx=image base loaded at 0x180041be4)

The point-format index used for the lookup is the arg5 stack argument the callers already pass in both builds; it is a static per-curve property (curve type at descriptor+4), not attacker-controlled blob data
For every elliptic-curve import reachable in the unpatched build the index is 2 and SymCryptEcpointFormatNumberofElements[2] == 2, identical to the hardcoded doubling

The size check is an equality gate returning STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER (0x800E) on mismatch; the buffer allocation size is taken from an internal key-object field ([rsi+3Ch]), not from the validated cbPublicKey
changed_functions.security_notes: SymCryptEckeySetValue is statically-linked SymCrypt library code. The change replaces a hardcoded element-count multiplier of 2 with a lookup into the SymCryptEcpointFormatNumberofElements table, using the point-format index the callers already pass. The table is pre-existing and already used by other SymCrypt routines in both builds. The index is a static per-curve value ((curve_type != 3) ? 2 : 1), the validated length (r9 = cbPublicKey) is caller-derived as 2*field_element_size rather than raw attacker input, and the internal buffer allocation uses [rsi+3Ch]. For all reachable short-Weierstrass imports the looked-up count is 2, identical to the previous behavior, so no reachable divergence or out-of-bounds condition is demonstrable. The generalization supports the additional point formats introduced by this build's post-quantum / composite-algorithm SymCrypt uplift (composite-encoding key routines and further ML-DSA / PQ-DSA functions are present only in the patched binary).
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED (SymCryptEckeySetValue @ 0x180041b98) - public-key size check:
0000000180041C68  test    r9, r9                 ; r9 = cbPublicKey (caller-derived = 2 * field_element_size)
0000000180041C6B  jz      short loc_180041C77
0000000180041C6D  mov     eax, [rsi+14h]         ; field_element_size
0000000180041C70  add     eax, eax               ; hardcoded element count 2
0000000180041C72  cmp     r9, rax
0000000180041C75  jnz     short loc_180041C44    ; STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER (0x800E)
0000000180041C77  mov     edi, [rsi+3Ch]         ; internal key-object alloc size (NOT cbPublicKey)
0000000180041C80  call    SymCryptCallbackAlloc

PATCHED (SymCryptEckeySetValue @ 0x180041b50) - same check via table lookup:
0000000180041BE4  lea     rcx, cs:180000000h     ; image base, loaded earlier
0000000180041C24  test    r9, r9
0000000180041C27  jz      short loc_180041C41
0000000180041C29  movsxd  rax, [rsp+0C8h+arg_28] ; arg5 = point-format index (static per-curve)
0000000180041C31  mov     eax, ds:rva SymCryptEcpointFormatNumberofElements[rcx+rax*4]
0000000180041C38  imul    eax, [rsi+14h]         ; field_element_size * element_count
0000000180041C3C  cmp     r9, rax
0000000180041C3F  jnz     short loc_180041C00    ; STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER (0x800E)
0000000180041C41  mov     edi, [rsi+3Ch]
0000000180041C4A  call    SymCryptCallbackAlloc

changed_functions.name: ImportEccKeyBlobWithoutParameters (sub_18004166c) / ImportEccKeyBlobWithoutParameters (sub_18004161c)
key_changes: Primary caller of SymCryptEckeySetValue. Builds a two-coordinate public-key buffer of 2*field_element_size (var_18) and passes it as cbPublicKey (r9)
Computes the point-format index in both builds as: 'cmp dword [r8+4], 3; setnz al; inc eax' (yields 2 for short-Weierstrass, 1 for Montgomery/type 3), passed as arg5
Patched build additionally stores a constant 2 in an added stack argument slot at 0x180041943 ('mov dword ptr [rsp+70h+var_48], 2'), consistent with a SymCrypt library signature revision
Call target is SymCryptEckeySetValue in both (0x180041b98 unpatched at call site 0x18004199c, 0x180041b50 patched at call site 0x180041954)
changed_functions.security_notes: The caller derives cbPublicKey as 2*field_element_size and passes a static per-curve point-format index. For the short-Weierstrass curves reachable here the index is 2, so the patched callee's table lookup returns the same value as the unpatched hardcoded doubling. No behavioral difference for reachable inputs.

changed_functions.name: ImportEccKeyBlobWithParameters (sub_180079c38) / ImportEccKeyBlobWithParameters (sub_18007a558)
key_changes: Second caller of SymCryptEckeySetValue. Same point-format index computation; passes the same added constant argument in the patched build
Pre-existing integer-overflow checks on blob-size arithmetic are present in both builds and unchanged (they are not the change)
changed_functions.security_notes: Alternative key import path. The overflow checks for blob-size arithmetic exist in both builds and are unchanged; the callee's table generalization applies to this path but is behaviorally identical for reachable short-Weierstrass imports.

changed_functions.name: SetEccPrivateKeyVal (sub_18007bab0) / SetEccPrivateKeyVal (sub_18007c3a8)
key_changes: Third caller of SymCryptEckeySetValue. Same point-format index computation and the same added constant argument in the patched build
changed_functions.security_notes: Additional key set path. Behaviorally identical to the other callers for reachable inputs.

key_changes: Large function (689 instructions, 149 basic blocks) with moderate changes
Related to ML-DSA and Composite-ML-DSA algorithm registration support added in this build
Algorithm name string comparisons for new post-quantum algorithm families

Patched version adds wcscmp checks for ECDSA_P256, ECDSA_P384, ECDSA_P521, ML-DSA, Composite-ML-DSA
Returns different algorithm descriptor pointers based on algorithm name
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature addition for post-quantum algorithm support, not a security fix.

changed_functions.name: MSCryptMlDsaGetProperty (sub_18007cd90) / MSCryptPqDsaFinalizeKeyPair (sub_18007d4f0)

References ML-DSA and Composite-ML-DSA blob tags (0x4d534b59='YKSM', 0x4d53414c='LASM')
New provider initialization paths for 0x3000c and 0x3000d algorithm types
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature addition for post-quantum algorithm property queries.

changed_functions.name: SymCryptDlkeySetValue (sub_180092860) / SymCryptDlkeySetValue (sub_18009588c)
key_changes: Register renaming: rdi->r14 for arg6, rbp->rdi for provider context (compiler re-allocation)
Call target address changes due to binary recompilation (SymCryptFdefRawSetValue sub_180026a30->sub_180027a70, etc.)
Minor control flow restructuring in flag bit testing (bits 0xc/0xd) but logic equivalent
changed_functions.security_notes: Register reallocation and recompilation artifacts. No behavioral change in security-relevant logic.

changed_functions.name: VsmOpenAlgorithmProvider (sub_1800482ec) / VsmOpenAlgorithmProvider (sub_18004856c)
key_changes: Provider initialization structure size increased from 0x3b8 to 0x3c8 (16 bytes larger)
Algorithm count table expanded from 0x33 to 0x35 entries (2 new algorithm types added)
Loop copy count changed from 2 to 3 iterations, accommodating additional provider fields
changed_functions.security_notes: Provider structure expansion for new ML-DSA/Composite-ML-DSA algorithm support.

changed_functions.name: SymCryptRsakeySetValueInternal (sub_18008e82c) / SymCryptRsakeySetValueInternal (sub_18008ff18)
key_changes: Register reallocation and call address changes from recompilation
No security-relevant logic changes

security_findings.entry_point: BCryptImportKeyPair (exported CNG API) -> provider dispatch -> MSCryptEccImportKeyPair (sub_18007b6e8) -> ImportEccKeyBlobWithoutParameters (sub_18004166c)
security_findings.description: In the statically-linked SymCrypt routine SymCryptEckeySetValue, the unpatched build validates the imported public-key length (r9 = cbPublicKey) against field_element_size * 2 using a hardcoded doubling at 0x180041c6d-0x180041c70 ('mov eax,[rsi+14h]; add eax,eax; cmp r9,rax'). The patched build instead reads the point-format index the callers already pass (arg5), indexes the pre-existing SymCryptEcpointFormatNumberofElements table with it, and multiplies field_element_size by the resulting element count at 0x180041c29-0x180041c38. This is not a reachable security issue: (1) the point-format index is a static per-curve property computed by the callers as (curve_type != 3) ? 2 : 1 (curve type at descriptor+4; value 3 is the Montgomery / X-only type), not attacker-controlled blob data; (2) the validated length r9 is caller-derived as 2*field_element_size (var_18 in the callers), not raw attacker input; (3) the buffer that receives key material is allocated from an internal key-object field [rsi+3Ch], independent of the validated length, and the size check only returns STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER (0x800E) on mismatch; (4) for every elliptic-curve import reachable in the unpatched build the curve is short-Weierstrass, so the index is 2 and SymCryptEcpointFormatNumberofElements[2] == 2, identical to the hardcoded doubling. The table is pre-existing and already used by other SymCrypt routines in both builds. The generalization supports the additional point formats used by this build's post-quantum / composite-algorithm SymCrypt uplift (composite-encoding key routines and additional ML-DSA / PQ-DSA functions are present only in the patched binary).
security_findings.trigger_conditions: No security-relevant trigger. Reaching SymCryptEckeySetValue via BCryptImportKeyPair and the ECC import path exercises the size check, but for reachable short-Weierstrass curves the patched table lookup returns the same value (2) as the unpatched hardcoded doubling. The check is an equality gate between two caller-derived quantities and does not gate an attacker-controlled copy length.
security_findings.primitive: None. No out-of-bounds read or write and no privilege-escalation primitive is demonstrable from this change in the unpatched build.
security_findings.assembly:
UNPATCHED SymCryptEckeySetValue @ 0x180041b98:
0x180041C68: test    r9, r9                 ; r9 = cbPublicKey (caller-derived = 2 * field_element_size)
0x180041C6B: jz      0x180041C77
0x180041C6D: mov     eax, [rsi+14h]         ; field_element_size
0x180041C70: add     eax, eax               ; hardcoded element count 2
0x180041C72: cmp     r9, rax
0x180041C75: jnz     0x180041C44            ; STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER (0x800E)
0x180041C77: mov     edi, [rsi+3Ch]         ; internal key-object alloc size (NOT cbPublicKey)
0x180041C80: call    SymCryptCallbackAlloc

PATCHED SymCryptEckeySetValue @ 0x180041b50:
0x180041BE4: lea     rcx, cs:180000000h     ; image base
0x180041C24: test    r9, r9
0x180041C27: jz      0x180041C41
0x180041C29: movsxd  rax, [rsp+0C8h+arg_28] ; arg5 = point-format index (static per-curve)
0x180041C31: mov     eax, ds:rva SymCryptEcpointFormatNumberofElements[rcx+rax*4]
0x180041C38: imul    eax, [rsi+14h]         ; field_element_size * element_count
0x180041C3C: cmp     r9, rax
0x180041C3F: jnz     0x180041C00            ; STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER (0x800E)
0x180041C41: mov     edi, [rsi+3Ch]
0x180041C4A: call    SymCryptCallbackAlloc
security_findings.notes: SymCryptEcpointFormatNumberofElements exists in both binaries and is already used by other SymCrypt routines in both builds (for example around 0x18002590f/0x180025a0e unpatched and 0x180025b9c/0x18002694f patched); the patch adds a single new use inside SymCryptEckeySetValue at 0x180041c31 (patched only). The three callers (ImportEccKeyBlobWithoutParameters sub_18004166c, ImportEccKeyBlobWithParameters sub_180079c38, SetEccPrivateKeyVal sub_18007bab0) compute the same static per-curve index in both builds; the patched callers additionally pass a constant 2 in an added stack argument slot, consistent with a SymCrypt library signature revision. Composite-encoding key routines (SymCryptEckeySetValueCompositeEncodingPk / ...Sk) and additional ML-DSA / PQ-DSA functions are absent from the unpatched binary and present in the patched binary.

summary: The most notable code change in this diff is inside the statically-linked SymCrypt routine SymCryptEckeySetValue (sub_180041b98 unpatched / sub_180041b50 patched), reached from the elliptic-curve key import path. The unpatched build validates the imported public-key length against field_element_size * 2 using a hardcoded doubling; the patched build reads the point-format index the callers already pass and looks the element count up in the pre-existing SymCryptEcpointFormatNumberofElements table instead. This is not a reachable security fix: the point-format index is a static per-curve property (Montgomery vs. short-Weierstrass), the validated length is caller-derived as 2*field_element_size rather than raw attacker input, and the buffer allocation uses an internal key-object field. For every elliptic-curve import reachable in the unpatched build the looked-up element count is 2, identical to the hardcoded doubling, so no reachable divergence or out-of-bounds condition is demonstrable. The change generalizes the routine for the additional point formats introduced by this build's post-quantum / composite-algorithm SymCrypt uplift (composite-encoding key routines and further ML-DSA / PQ-DSA functions appear only in the patched binary). Verdict: No security-relevant change.
